name: El Picacho Trail (PR-A 117)
name_original: Sendero El Picacho – trail running circular desde Taberno (PR-A 117)
description: Run the moderate 5.3-mile Sendero El Picacho circular trail (PR-A 117) from Taberno, featuring diverse terrain and scenic viewpoints.
region_intro: Jogging around Taberno offers a diverse experience through its unique natural landscapes. The region is characterized by a mix of semi-desert terrain and lush ravines, providing varied environments for running. Routes often traverse wide ramblas and narrow ravines, with some areas featuring "badlands" topography of eroded ravines and sculpted clay hills. This creates a dynamic and visually engaging backdrop for outdoor activities. Best jogging routes around Taberno The most popular jogging route is El Picacho Trail (PR-A 117), a 5.3 miles (8.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 1 minute to complete. This route offers moderate climbs and panoramic views across the Almanzora basin. Another top favourite among local runners is Ruined House loop from Huércal-Overa, a moderate 8.9 miles (14.3 km) path. This route winds through varied terrains, including semi-desert stretches and areas with unique geological formations. Local runners also love the Ruined House loop from Huércal-Overa, an 8.7 miles (14.0 km) trail leading through diverse landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 29 minutes. Jogging in Taberno is defined by semi-desert terrain, lush ravines, and badlands topography. The network of routes offers options for easy, moderate, and challenging running experiences. The routes in Taberno are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from more than 1 review. More than 30 runners have used komoot to explore Taberno's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Taberno?
A: There are 9 dedicated running routes around Taberno documented on komoot, offering a range of experiences from easy to difficult. These routes allow you to explore the region's unique landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes in Taberno?
A: Taberno's running routes feature a diverse mix of terrains. You'll encounter semi-desert stretches, lush ravines, wide ramblas (dry riverbeds), and narrow paths. Some areas, particularly those influenced by the nearby Tabernas Desert, showcase unique 'badlands' topography with eroded ravines and sculpted clay hills, providing a visually dynamic backdrop for your run.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners in Taberno?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy running route available. For a gentler experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40379321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Welcome Stranger&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 3.9 km (2.4 miles) with minimal elevation gain, making it ideal for beginners or a relaxed jog.

Q: Can I find challenging running trails in the Taberno area?
A: While most routes are moderate, some offer more challenging elements. The region's varied topography, including climbs towards viewpoints like Mirador las Morras, can provide a good workout. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985188029/el-picacho-trail-pr-a-117" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Picacho Trail (PR-A 117)&lt;/a&gt; includes moderate climbs and covers over 8.5 km, offering a rewarding challenge.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Absolutely! The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985188029/el-picacho-trail-pr-a-117" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Picacho Trail (PR-A 117)&lt;/a&gt; is known for its panoramic views across the Almanzora basin, extending as far as the beaches at Garrucha and the town of Huércal-Overa. Incorporating climbs into your run often leads to these scenic rewards.

Q: What are some interesting natural features or landmarks I might see while running in Taberno?
A: The Taberno area is rich in natural beauty. You might encounter unique natural monuments such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2547310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Bárbara Fountain&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2093651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salty Spring&lt;/a&gt;, or the distinctive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2470738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turtle Rock&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5977435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rambla del Aceituno&lt;/a&gt; also offers a scenic gorge to explore. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5313137" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;town of Taberno&lt;/a&gt; itself is a highlight.

Q: Are the running trails in Taberno suitable for families?
A: While specific family-friendly routes are not explicitly detailed, the presence of easy trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40379321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Welcome Stranger&lt;/a&gt; suggests options for families. The 'Las Morras' recreational area, a starting point for some trails, is well-equipped with amenities like playgrounds, making it a good base for family outings.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running routes in Taberno?
A: Many natural trails in the Taberno region are generally dog-friendly, especially those traversing ramblas and open semi-desert areas. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land, and to check for any specific local regulations or signage on individual routes.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Taberno?
A: Yes, several routes in the area are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14603717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruined House loop from Huércal-Overa&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular path, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21837860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Huércal-Overa&lt;/a&gt;, offering convenient options for runners who prefer to start and end in the same location.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Taberno?
A: The best time for running in Taberno is typically during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May). Summers can be very hot due to the semi-desert climate. During the cooler seasons, the weather is more pleasant for outdoor activities, allowing you to fully enjoy the diverse landscapes.

Q: Is there parking available near the running trailheads in Taberno?
A: Yes, many trailheads in and around Taberno, especially those starting from recreational areas like 'Las Morras', offer parking facilities. For routes originating from towns like Huércal-Overa, you can usually find public parking options nearby. It's always a good idea to check the specific starting point of your chosen route for parking information.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Taberno?
A: The running routes in Taberno are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.0 stars. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from semi-desert to lush ravines, and the unique 'badlands' topography, which provides a dynamic and visually engaging experience. The panoramic views from higher points are also frequently highlighted as a rewarding aspect.
tips: The El Picacho Trail in Taberno, known as PR-A 117, is a circular route of approximately 8.1 km. It begins in Taberno's Town Hall Square and continues along Avenida de Andalucía to the first information panel. Along the way, you can enjoy panoramic views, diverse vegetation, and interesting geological formations.

The Rambla del Aceituno in Taberno, Almería, is a popular hiking trail that offers a journey through stunning natural landscapes. The route is of moderate difficulty and allows you to enjoy the local flora and fauna, as well as panoramic views of the Sierra de las Estancias.

It is ideal for a leisurely walk and for those looking to connect with nature.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Sendero El Picacho – circular trail?
faqs.answer: The komoot tour data estimates the duration for this 8.57 km trail at approximately 1 hour. However, your actual jogging time will depend on your pace and fitness level.

faqs.question: Is the Sendero El Picacho trail suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is officially rated as 'low-medium' difficulty and described as having 'friendly terrain' with a 'simple circular path'. While there are 'little slopes' and 'descents', it's generally accessible for a wide range of users, including beginner joggers.

faqs.question: Where is the trailhead for the Sendero El Picacho – circular trail and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Taberno. While specific parking details aren't provided, the El Picacho Trail (PR-A 117) is a well-known route, suggesting parking options would be available in or near Taberno. The Rambla del Aceituno is also a key feature along the route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Sendero El Picacho trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied terrain. It begins with an paved section, then transitions to dirt tracks and paths through agricultural terraces. You'll encounter geological features like reddish conglomerates and sandstones, cross the Barranco del Tollo via an earthen dyke, and traverse sections of the Rambla del Aceituno with its large conglomerate flagstones. Portions also pass through a canyon with high walls of red conglomerates.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and vegetation can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a contrast between semi-desert characteristics and lush ravines. Expect views of diverse Mediterranean vegetation, including hastate orach, albardine, esparto grass, fennel, and mountain rue, alongside pine tree repopulations and extensive almond plantations. The Rambla del Aceituno area is particularly scenic with unique rock formations and a waterfall.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the semi-desert characteristics mentioned, it's advisable to visit during cooler months, such as spring or autumn, to avoid the intense summer heat. These seasons also offer pleasant temperatures for jogging and vibrant vegetation.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, several points of interest enhance the experience. You'll pass the Calvario chapel early on. The Mirador de Las Morras offers panoramic views of Taberno and features a fountain. The Rambla del Aceituno is a highlight for its geological formations and waterfall. There's also an accessible observatory of fauna and flora, and a botanic park near Taberno.

faqs.question: Is the Sendero El Picacho – circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail research does not explicitly state whether dogs are allowed. It's best to check local regulations or signage at the trailhead in Taberno regarding pet policies before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to access the trail?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of permits or entrance fees required for the Sendero El Picacho – circular trail (PR-A 117). It appears to be freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like water or restrooms available along the trail?
faqs.answer: The Mirador de Las Morras, a viewpoint along the trail, is noted as having a fountain with potable water and also serves as a recreational spot. This would be a good place for a brief stop and to refill water bottles. Restroom facilities are not explicitly mentioned for the trail itself, but might be available in Taberno or at the Mirador.

faqs.question: Does the Sendero El Picacho trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is primarily the Sendero del Picacho (PR-A 117), covering 99% of the route. It also passes through sections of the Cerro Alto trail for about 45% of its length.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter on the Sendero El Picacho trail?
faqs.answer: While specific wildlife isn't detailed, the presence of an 'Observatory of fauna and flora' along the trail suggests opportunities for wildlife viewing. You can expect to see various birds and small mammals typical of Mediterranean semi-desert and ravine environments.
